message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: I'm adding case_sensitive param to groupby() filter in src/jinja2/filters.py. What file(s) does your feature touch? If also filters.py, let's coordinate on non-overlapping regions (I'll only touch do_groupby/do_agroupby functions).

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: My feature (with_counts param for groupby filter) only touches the do_groupby/sync_do_groupby functions in filters.py. If your feature also touches filters.py, let's split by function/line-range within the file, or serialize edits (one of us finishes and shares diff, then the other rebases on top). Please share which functions/sections your feature touches.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Confirmed: my case_sensitive param and your with_counts param both touch sync_do_groupby/do_groupby (and likely the async variant). True overlap, so let's serialize instead of splitting. Proposal: I'll implement my change first (small, isolated to the sort-key/grouping logic) and send DONE with a summary of the exact signature change. You then rebase your with_counts param on top of my version so the final function has both params. If you're already further along, say so and I'll rebase on yours instead - just reply which of us goes first.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: We both need to touch sync_do_groupby/do_groupby directly (same lines), so true disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: serialize edits instead. I'll go first — add with_counts param (simple, additive: optional bool, yields (grouper, list, count) tuples when True). I'll send DONE with the exact diff when finished, then you rebase and add case_sensitive on top in a way that composes with with_counts (e.g. both params independently controlling the sort key / tuple shape). Please ACCEPT if that ordering works, or propose you go first instead — either is fine, I just want us not editing concurrently.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Accepted. You go first with with_counts, send DONE when finished, then I'll rebase case_sensitive on top so both params compose. I'll wait until I receive your DONE before editing.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Confirmed, I'll go first. Implementing with_counts now, will send DONE with exact signature/behavior summary when finished.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished with_counts. Summary of change to rebase on: added new NamedTuple class _GroupTupleWithCount(grouper, list, count) right after _GroupTuple. Both sync_do_groupby and do_groupby(async) gained a new keyword-only-by-convention param 'with_counts: bool = False' appended AFTER 'default' (so signature is (environment, value, attribute, default=None, with_counts=False)). When with_counts=True, the function builds a list of _GroupTupleWithCount(key, values_list, len(values_list)) instead of the normal _GroupTuple(key, values_list) - branches early with an if/return, so the original default-path code (used when with_counts=False) is UNCHANGED except the async version now computes 'as_list = await auto_to_list(value)' once before branching (both branches use as_list) instead of inlining auto_to_list(value) in the sorted() call - functionally identical, just avoids duplicating the await. Return type annotations changed to Union[List[Tuple[grouper,list]], List[Tuple[grouper,list,int]]]. Please add your case_sensitive param after with_counts (or wherever fits) and keep the with_counts branch intact. Let me know if you want the diff pasted directly.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Heads up: my local copy of filters.py still shows the original (pre-with_counts) code - looks like we're in separate sandboxes/worktrees rather than a shared filesystem, so I can't see your literal edits. Could you paste the exact diff (git diff output) for sync_do_groupby/do_groupby so I can splice case_sensitive in at the exact same lines and avoid structural drift? Your prose summary was clear but I want byte-exact compatibility.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished case_sensitive param for groupby(). Since our sandboxes turned out to be separate copies (my file never showed your with_counts edits), I implemented case_sensitive against the original base sync_do_groupby/do_groupby, factored the core logic into a new helper _group_by(value, expr, case_sensitive) that both sync/async call. Signature: (environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False). When case_sensitive=False (default), sorting/grouping key uses ignore_case(expr(item)), but the emitted grouper preserves original case from the first item in each group. All 837 existing tests still pass. If you do end up merging our two diffs by hand, the with_counts branch and my case_sensitive branch are independent axes (one controls key normalization, the other controls output tuple shape) so they should compose by adding both params to the signature and applying with_counts on top of _group_by's output tuples.
